steps.title: Find the grammar problem
steps.content: The blank must connect two independent clauses: "A ray diagram reveals how this ______ the hole's small size restricts light to a single ray" and "all light passing through the hole can only arrive at a single destination." Joining two independent clauses with a comma alone is a comma splice — a violation of Standard English punctuation rules.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, keep the one that fixes the comma splice
steps.content:
Substitute each option and check whether the result produces a grammatically complete sentence or a comma splice/run-on:
  • Choice A — 'works because': creates a run-on — two sentences fused with no punctuation or conjunction between them. ✗
  • Choice B — 'works. Because': the period ends the first sentence; "Because the hole's small size restricts light to a single ray" becomes a dependent clause that correctly introduces the next independent clause. ✓
  • Choice C — 'works, it's because': the comma joins two independent clauses — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — 'works: it's because': "it's because" creates a second independent clause after the colon, then the sentence continues with another independent clause after the comma — still a comma splice. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
